description:
You are an infrastructure engineer writing a quick smoke-test for a local
    “mock API” file that will later be served over HTTP.  
    Everything you need already exists inside the home directory; **do not try to
    access the public Internet.**

    Goal  
    -----  
    1. Issue a single, one-liner *curl* command that:  
       • Reads the file located at
         file:///home/user/mock_api/users.json  
       • Saves the body exactly as it is to
         /home/user/provision_output/users_response.json  
       • Writes a tiny log line to
         /home/user/provision_logs/api_test.log that contains only:  

         STATUS=SUCCESS|||SIZE=<bytes>

         where <bytes> is the number of bytes *curl* actually downloaded.  
    2. The command must be self-contained: you may chain sub-commands with “&&”,
       but everything must be entered on **one** terminal line.  
    3. The required directory structure must exist when the command finishes:
       /home/user/provision_output/ and /home/user/provision_logs/.

    Exact output formats the grader will verify  
    -------------------------------------------  
    • /home/user/provision_output/users_response.json **must contain exactly**  
      {"users":[{"id":1,"name":"Alice"},{"id":2,"name":"Bob"}]}  

      (no trailing newline).  

    • /home/user/provision_logs/api_test.log **must consist of one line** that
      ends with a newline character “\n” and matches exactly:  

      STATUS=SUCCESS|||SIZE=57\n

      – “STATUS=SUCCESS” (all caps, no spaces)  
      – the literal triple-bar separator “|||”  
      – “SIZE=” followed by the byte count reported by *curl -w %{size_download}*
        (for the given file it must be 57).  
      – A single LF newline at the very end.

    Success criteria  
    ----------------  
    The automated grader will:  
    1. Check that both required directories exist.  
    2. Verify that /home/user/provision_output/users_response.json is present and
       its contents are **bit-for-bit** identical to the string above.  
    3. Verify that /home/user/provision_logs/api_test.log exists and contains the
       exact string “STATUS=SUCCESS|||SIZE=57\n”.

    Hints (do not print these)  
    --------------------------  
    • The *curl* “file://” protocol works on regular files.  
    • “-o” saves the body, “-s” silences progress, “-w” can emit a custom string
      containing “%{size_download}”.  
    • Use “mkdir -p” to build directory trees and “&&” to keep it all in one
      command line.
truth:
Initial state supplied to the agent
    ----------------------------------
    1. Directory: /home/user/mock_api/
    2. File: /home/user/mock_api/users.json
       Exact contents (57 bytes, no newline):
       {"users":[{"id":1,"name":"Alice"},{"id":2,"name":"Bob"}]}

    Expected artifacts after successful completion
    ----------------------------------------------
    1. Directory: /home/user/provision_output/      (must exist)
    2. Directory: /home/user/provision_logs/        (must exist)

    3. File: /home/user/provision_output/users_response.json
       Exact contents (identical copy of the source file):
       {"users":[{"id":1,"name":"Alice"},{"id":2,"name":"Bob"}]}

    4. File: /home/user/provision_logs/api_test.log
       Exact contents (including trailing LF):
       STATUS=SUCCESS|||SIZE=57\n
